Tests for a SyntaxError when evaluating invalid byte sequences in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a test script that verifies the behavior of the `eval` function when it encounters invalid byte sequences. It attempts to evaluate a string containing invalid byte sequences using `eval`, which should throw a `SyntaxError`. The `try` block contains the `eval` call and an `assert` statement that should not execute if the error is correctly thrown. The `catch` block captures the error and uses `assert` to confirm that the error is an instance of `SyntaxError`. This script is used to ensure that the JavaScript engine correctly handles syntax errors during evaluation.
